title: Method for identifying non-responders to first line of tuberculosis drug regime
abstract: The present invention provides a method for identifying non-responder to a first line of tuberculosis drug regime, which have a prospective risk of development of drug resistant tuberculosis (DR-TB) infection in plurality of subjects by using differential levels of RNA signatures therein. The method comprises identifying differential levels of RNA signatures by analysing RNA library; quantitatively analysing the detected small non-coding RNAs for identifying exhibited differential levels of RNA signatures in plurality of subjects, and creating expression profiles for plurality of subjects; comparing the expression profiles of the plurality of subjects to identify responders and non-responders based on the differential levels of the RNA signatures; and validating the expression profiles of the non-responders against the expression profiles of the responders for determining the prospective risk of development of drug resistant tuberculosis infection. Moreover, the present invention provides a diagnostic kit to be used with the aforementioned method.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for identifying a non-responder to a first line of tuberculosis drug regime, which have a prospective risk of development of drug resistant tuberculosis (DR-TB) infection in a plurality of subjects by using differential levels of RNA signatures in the plurality of subjects, the method comprising : (i) identifying the differential levels of the RNA signatures by analysing an RNA library, wherein analysing the RNA library comprises: - filtering raw sequence reads of the RNA library based on a quality score; - removing the adaptor sequences from the filtered raw sequence reads; - preparing a FASTA file comprising reads obtained after removal of the adaptor sequences; - performing a BLAST search using the prepared FASTA file against a predefined database of human small non-coding RNA sequences and eliminating the sequence reads which are not related to the predefined database; - aligning the sequence reads remaining after elimination of the sequence reads which are not related to the predefined database; - allowing a 6-nucleotide overhang at the 3' end and a 3-nucleotide overhang at the 5' end of the remaining sequence reads for DICER processing; and - normalizing read counts for each detected small non-coding RNA to the total number of mapped small non-coding RNAs to quantify each of the detected small non-coding RNA; (ii) quantitatively analysing the detected small non-coding RNAs for identifying exhibited differential levels of the RNA signatures in the plurality of subjects, and creating expression profiles for the plurality of subjects; 

 (iii) comparing the expression profiles of the plurality of subjects to identify responders and non-responders based on the differential levels of the RNA signatures; and (iv) validating the expression profiles of the non-responders against the expression profiles of the responders, for determining the prospective risk of development of drug resistant tuberculosis infection. 2. A method of claim 1, wherein the plurality of subjects includes at least one healthy control, responder and non-responder. 3. A method of claim 2, wherein the healthy control is a person with no clinical symptoms and diagnosed negative for tuberculosis infection. 4. A method of claim 2, wherein the responder is a patient showing response to the first line of treatment evident from a decreased level of bacterial load. 5. A method of claim 1, wherein the non-responder is a patient not showing response to the first line of treatment evident from a continued presence of bacterial load. 6. A method of claim 1, wherein prior to step (i) the method further comprises: (i) collecting at least one biological sample from the plurality of subjects; (ii) isolating RNA from the at least one collected sample; (iii) creating an RNA library, wherein the created RNA library comprises a sub-combination of small non-coding RNAs; and (iv) sequencing the created RNA library. 7. A method of claim 6, wherein the RNA library is created using chemically-modified DNA adaptors at both the ends of the isolated RNA. 8. A method of claim 7, wherein the chemically-modified DNA adaptors are designed to capture small non-coding RNAs with a 5' phosphate group. 9. A diagnostic kit for detecting RNA signatures in a biological sample for identifying a non-responder to a first line of tuberculosis drug regime, which have a prospective risk of development of drug resistant tuberculosis (DR-TB) infection in a subject, the diagnostic kit comprising : (i) a device for collecting the biological sample from the subject; (ii) a microfluidic cartridge-based assay device for reading and analysing the results from the collected biological sample; (iii) reagents required for isolation of RNA sample from the collected biological sample; (iv) reagents required for PCR amplification of the isolated RNA sample; and (v) at least one small non-coding RNA expression panels for validating the expression profiles of the non-responders against the expression profiles of the responders. 10. A diagnostic kit of claim 9, wherein the reagents required for isolation of RNA comprises an extraction reagent, Chloroform, Isoamyl alcohol, Isopropyl alcohol, LiCI, DNAase and sterile water. 11. A diagnostic kit of claim 9, wherein the reagents required for PCR amplification comprises Reverse transcriptase, Oligo (dT) primers, dNTPs, PCR buffer, LiCI and sterile water. 12. A diagnostic kit of claim 9, wherein the diagnostic kit further comprises: a syringe for obtaining the biological sample from the subject, and a storage unit for storing the obtained biological sample.
